title: antennaria rosea subsp. arida
intro: Antennaria rosea subsp. arida, also known as dry leafy arctic cottongrass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Arctic tundra regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Antennaria rosea subsp. arida include its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, making it a useful remedy for minor cuts and scrapes. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions or decoctions, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally to support immune function.
